g.text: HTTP/1.1 Head-of-Line Blocking: Resp2 must wait for Resp1 even if Resp2 is ready first
HTTP/2 multiplexing: each request gets a stream ID; responses can arrive out-of-order
Simple implementation: response_queue[] per conn; dequeue only when front is sent
Both requests arrive as one byte stream — no delimiter between them
MUST send Resp1 before Resp2 — TCP has no framing
read_len | size_t | total bytes in buffer
